summary: Complete testing of Push Notification system for SDM Fintech Ghana. Backend: 100% (18/18 tests passed). Frontend: All UI components verified - Push Status cards, New Notification form with 'Send as Push Notification' checkbox, and proper button state changes. OneSignal is in SIMULATION mode (credentials not configured) - all push operations succeed with 'simulated: true' response.

design_issues.screen: Contact form select and Notifications table
issues: Minor React hydration warning about <span> in <tbody> and <option> in <select> - cosmetic only, doesn't affect functionality

passed_tests: GET /api/sdm/admin/push/stats - Returns is_configured=false, active_devices, total_devices, by_platform, by_user_type
GET /api/sdm/admin/push/stats - Requires admin authentication (401 without token)
POST /api/sdm/admin/push/test - Returns is_configured=false with 'not configured' message
POST /api/sdm/admin/push/send?recipient_type=all - Sends simulated push to all users
POST /api/sdm/admin/push/send?recipient_type=clients - Sends simulated push to clients
POST /api/sdm/admin/push/send?recipient_type=merchants - Sends simulated push to merchants
POST /api/sdm/admin/push/send with URL - Includes action URL in notification
POST /api/sdm/admin/push/send with data payload - Custom data payload supported
POST /api/sdm/user/push/register - Registers device with player_id, platform, device_model
POST /api/sdm/user/push/register - Re-registering same player_id updates existing (status='updated')
GET /api/sdm/user/push/devices - Returns list of registered devices with count
POST /api/sdm/user/push/unregister - Unregisters device (marks is_active=false)
POST /api/sdm/user/push/unregister - Returns success=false for nonexistent device
User push endpoints require authentication (401 without token)
Push stats reflect registered devices after registration
POST /api/sdm/merchant/push/register - Merchant device registration works
POST /api/sdm/merchant/push/unregister - Merchant device unregistration works
Push stats by_user_type shows client and merchant counts
UI: Push Status card shows 'Not Configured' (amber color)
UI: Active Devices card shows count
UI: Clients count card shows 0
UI: Merchants count card shows 0
UI: New Notification button opens form
UI: 'Send as Push Notification' checkbox present with label
UI: Checkbox toggle changes button from 'Send In-App' to 'Send Push'
UI: Notification form has Recipients, Type, Priority dropdowns
UI: Notification form has Title, Message, Action URL fields
seed_data_creation: Created test push device registrations for both client and merchant users. Push notifications sent during testing are stored in notifications collection with type='push'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push notification system fully tested in SIMULATION mode. All endpoints work correctly - admin can view push stats, send push notifications (simulated), and test system status. Users/merchants can register/unregister devices for push. OneSignal credentials (ONESIGNAL_APP_ID and ONESIGNAL_API_KEY) are empty in .env - system gracefully handles this by returning simulated responses. When real credentials are added, actual push notifications will be sent to OneSignal API.

mocked_integrations.OneSignal Push Notification API: MOCKED/SIMULATED - No real push notifications sent. All push operations return simulated: true until ONESIGNAL_APP_ID and ONESIGNAL_API_KEY are configured in backend/.env

features_verified.admin_push_stats: ✅ Working - Returns is_configured, active_devices, total_devices, by_platform, by_user_type
features_verified.admin_push_test: ✅ Working - Returns configuration status and helpful message
features_verified.ui_push_status_cards: ✅ Working - Shows is_configured status, active_devices, clients, merchants
features_verified.ui_push_checkbox: ✅ Working - 'Send as Push Notification' checkbox in notification form
features_verified.ui_button_state_change: ✅ Working - Button changes from 'Send In-App' to 'Send Push'
